A01.reason: Engineering-only migration verification is explicitly outside the deployed consumer behavior contract.

A02.reason: Deletes the temporary recall row, reads through RecallRepository, and proves the read creates nothing.

A03.reason: Start and repeated start exercise production upsert and linkage.

A04.reason: Starts empty, selects a fixture-only queue, sends, and advances.

A05.reason: Restricts eligibility to two fixtures and verifies the short queue.

A06.reason: Makes every fixture ineligible and verifies no queue or send.

A07.reason: Verifies inactive, cooldown, and priority ordering with deterministic fixture selection.

B01.reason: Checks each accepted fixture learning mutation and cursor commit.

B02.reason: Delivers a complete queue in order and verifies wrap to zero.

B03.reason: Recorder false is compared against exact direct-DB evidence.

B04.reason: Raised transport exception is wrapped and rolled back.

B05.reason: Disabled and null-chat states are independently checked.

B06.reason: Inactive user is excluded and direct delivery is asserted as a no-op.

B07.reason: One inactive queued fixture is removed/refilled before a valid send.

B08.reason: All queued fixtures and replacement pool are made inactive.

B09.reason: The production delivery adapter is clock-controlled at before/start/inside/end/after boundary partitions.

C01.reason: Production command dispatch enables the active linked fixture profile.

C02.reason: Repeated start preserves queue/cursor and one state row.

C04.reason: Missing, malformed, and inactive linkage are automated; duplicate linkage is unsupported by the production unique constraint and remains visible in this reason.

C05.reason: Stop persists disabled state while command evidence retains the queue.

C06.reason: State response is recorded and the database is unchanged.

C07.reason: Unknown, non-command, no-message, missing-username, and missing-chat updates are no-ops.

C08.reason: Remove and postpone without replies return guidance without mutation.

C09.reason: Unparsable reply variants are checked for both commands.

C10.reason: Unknown fixture-prefixed word returns not-found and rolls back.

C11.reason: Known fixture outside the queue returns not-in-selection with exact state equality.

D01.reason: Postpone before cursor checks compaction and logical-next preservation.

D02.reason: At-cursor and after-cursor positions are checked independently.

D03.reason: Single eligible fixture exposes immediate self-refill and requires cursor zero.

D04.reason: Alternative fixtures refill to target without the postponed ID.

D05.reason: Queued remove checks inactive/low-priority vocabulary, compaction, and refill.

D06.reason: Soft remove of an absent fixture leaves queue/cursor unchanged.

D07.reason: Enough alternatives produce a disjoint full queue and cursor zero.

D08.reason: Exactly two alternatives produce a unique short queue.

D09.reason: Cooldown-blocking all fixtures produces an empty queue.

E01.reason: Endpoint-equivalent shared transaction deletes queued fixture and refills.

E02.reason: Not-queued fixture deletion preserves queue and state.

E03.reason: Deletion succeeds with no recall row and does not create one.

E04.reason: Missing and available foreign-owned vocabulary IDs are ownership-protected.

E05.reason: Synthetic endpoint-boundary refill failure rolls queue and deletion back.

E06.reason: Before/at/after cursor deletion is inspected inside transactions then rolled back.

F01.reason: Temporary missing, malformed, and valid offset file behavior is checked.

F02.reason: Ordered in-memory batch advances to max update ID plus one.

F03.reason: A real PostgreSQL division-by-zero aborts the first update before a later valid update.

F04.reason: Synthetic offset write failure follows a committed command and preserves the old cursor.

G01.reason: Two independent sessions deliver with bounded timeout and distinct cursor words.

G02.reason: Independent PostgreSQL sessions hold delivery at transport and prove start, stop, bump, postpone, Telegram remove, web soft delete, and hard delete wait for the aggregate lock.

G03.reason: Enumeration, independent-session deactivation, and locked delivery reproduce the race.

G04.reason: Two independent sessions concurrently enable the same username.

G05.reason: Independent delete/refill transactions verify deleted ID cannot reappear.

H01.reason: Teacher words are compared with exact persisted queue order.

H02.reason: Consumer-visible multi-state eligibility is covered; request-session and query-count instrumentation are explicitly outside this contract.

H03.reason: When foreign vocabulary exists, read/delete/queue operations are attempted with target-user ownership and fingerprinted.

I01.reason: Finally restores exact user/state/queue rows and proves fixture absence plus both isolation fingerprints.

I02.reason: Scenario exceptions are retained as failures while the same finally restoration path runs; recovery evidence is reported separately.

J01.reason: Start refreshes a changed chat ID without changing queue or cursor.

J02.reason: Stop followed by start retains and re-enables the same aggregate.

J03.reason: A renamed profile rejects the old Telegram username and accepts the new username for the existing user state.

J04.reason: Stop against an active linked user without a recall row creates one disabled empty state.

J05.reason: State output covers enabled/disabled and persisted queue order.

J06.reason: Missing usernames, unlinked profiles, inactive accounts, malformed updates, and no-op text are covered.

K01.reason: MarkdownV2-significant text and optional examples are asserted against the exact production HTTP payload.

K02.reason: Telegram ok false, malformed JSON, HTTP error, timeout, and transport error all reject delivery.

K03.reason: One user's delivery exception is isolated and later users are still invoked in deterministic order.

K04.reason: Accepted delivery advances learning metadata and cursor once through PostgreSQL.

K05.reason: Absent eligibility partitions and stale/entirely invalid queues send nothing or repair before sending.

L01.reason: Queued phrase, translation, and example edits preserve membership/cursor and reach Teacher and delivery consumers.

L02.reason: Production create and reactivation do not rewrite the current queue and the created eligible word is available to the next bump.

L03.reason: Priority and candidate-pool changes affect future selection while the persisted queue is independently asserted stable.

L04.reason: Queued and nonqueued hard deletion plus cursor-relative positions are covered through endpoint-equivalent orchestration.

L05.reason: Teacher context is reloaded after delivery, postpone, and remove and compared with persisted order.

L06.reason: Text/image forwarding, best-effort context failure, and bounded untrusted-data formatting are covered with deterministic agent and OCR recorders.

M01.reason: Unexpectedly unordered responses are processed by ascending update ID and offset uses max ID plus one.

M02.reason: Malformed offset, PostgreSQL-aborting update recovery, and offset-write failure are exercised.

M03.reason: Duplicate idempotent stop after offset failure leaves exact state unchanged.

M04.reason: A new worker instance reads the persisted offset and leaves database recall state intact.

M05.reason: Polling request, API ok false, malformed JSON, network failure, and empty response preserve offset.

N01.reason: Delivery is serialized with start, stop, and bump.

N02.reason: Delivery is serialized with postpone and Telegram remove.

N03.reason: Delivery is serialized with endpoint-equivalent web soft and hard delete.

N04.reason: Account deactivation is rechecked after enumeration and concurrent start remains one valid aggregate.

N05.reason: All queue and vocabulary operations remain ownership-scoped; username change is an authorization linkage update, not a recall mutation lock.
